entries.title: GMV protection alarm H5
entries.meaning: Preliminary diagnostic entry for a Gree GMV H5 alarm. Verify the exact meaning against the service manual for the installed model before concluding.
likelyCauses: Outdoor unit protection condition reported by the control board.
Abnormal supply voltage, phase condition, or wiring issue.
Compressor or inverter drive protection condition requiring qualified diagnosis.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm installed equipment identity
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Record the outdoor unit model, GMV series, serial plate data, and controller-displayed error code.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Manufacturer, series, model, and displayed H5 code are confirmed before diagnosis continues.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Stop classification and obtain the exact model information.

diagnosticSteps.title: Check electrical supply condition
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should measure supply voltage and phase condition at the service point using approved procedures.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Supply values are within the equipment nameplate and local electrical requirements.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Correct the electrical supply issue through qualified service before resetting operation.

diagnosticSteps.title: Inspect protection-related wiring
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Inspect compressor, inverter, control-board, and protection-sensor connections for visible damage, loose terminals, or water ingress.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Connections are secure and no visible damage is found.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Repair damaged wiring or components according to the manufacturer procedure.

requiredMeasurements.name: Supply voltage
requiredMeasurements.description: Measured line voltage and phase balance at the unit service point.

requiredMeasurements.name: Operating current
requiredMeasurements.description: Measured compressor or inverter input current where the service procedure requires it.

safetyNotes: Electrical, compressor, inverter, refrigerant, and chiller protection checks must be performed by a qualified technician.
Keep safety switches, protection inputs, current protection, pressure protection, flow protection, and controller safeguards active during diagnosis.
De-energize and lock out equipment where the service procedure requires it, and follow the manufacturer manual and local safety rules.

source.notes: Seeded deterministic diagnostic guidance. Not manually page-verified.
limitations: Use as preliminary diagnostic guidance only.
Verify against the exact service manual for the installed equipment family before final conclusion.

entries.title: GMV communication or configuration alarm C7
entries.meaning: Preliminary diagnostic entry for a Gree GMV C7 alarm. Confirm the exact model-specific meaning from the applicable manual.
likelyCauses: Indoor/outdoor communication fault or address/configuration mismatch.
Damaged communication wiring or incorrect polarity.
Controller or board communication issue requiring qualified service.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm network scope
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Record which controller, indoor unit, and outdoor unit display the C7 alarm.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: The affected communication segment is identified.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Map the connected units before replacing parts.

diagnosticSteps.title: Inspect communication wiring
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should inspect communication terminals, polarity, shielding, and continuity according to the installation manual.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Communication wiring is intact and connected to the correct terminals.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Correct wiring defects and re-test communication.

diagnosticSteps.title: Check addressing and configuration
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Verify controller addressing and unit configuration against the commissioning records and service manual.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Addresses and configuration match the installed system topology.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Correct configuration through the supported commissioning procedure.

requiredMeasurements.name: Communication bus voltage
requiredMeasurements.description: Measured at the communication terminals when required by the service manual.

requiredMeasurements.name: Cable continuity
requiredMeasurements.description: Continuity and short-circuit check for the communication cable with power isolated.

safetyNotes: Electrical, compressor, inverter, refrigerant, and chiller protection checks must be performed by a qualified technician.
Keep safety switches, protection inputs, current protection, pressure protection, flow protection, and controller safeguards active during diagnosis.
De-energize and lock out equipment where the service procedure requires it, and follow the manufacturer manual and local safety rules.

source.notes: Seeded deterministic diagnostic guidance. Not manually page-verified.
limitations: Use as preliminary diagnostic guidance only.
Verify against the exact service manual for the installed equipment family before final conclusion.

entries.title: GMV preliminary alarm E1
entries.meaning: Preliminary diagnostic guidance for a Gree GMV E1 alarm. Some Gree equipment families associate E1-style alarms with pressure-related protection, but the exact meaning must be verified against the service manual for the installed model.
likelyCauses: Pressure-related protection condition reported by the outdoor unit controls.
Restricted outdoor airflow, condenser heat rejection issue, or fan operation issue.
Refrigerant circuit condition, pressure sensor issue, or control-board input requiring qualified diagnosis.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm model and displayed code
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Record the outdoor unit model, GMV series, operating mode, ambient condition, and the displayed E1 code before classification.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: The installed equipment identity and displayed code are confirmed.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Stop classification and obtain the exact model and controller information.

diagnosticSteps.title: Check heat rejection conditions
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should inspect outdoor coil cleanliness, airflow path, fan operation, and installation clearances according to the service manual.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Outdoor heat rejection path is clear and fan operation is normal for the operating mode.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Correct airflow or fan-related issues through qualified service procedures before drawing a final conclusion.

diagnosticSteps.title: Compare pressure readings with service limits
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should measure relevant refrigerant pressures only where the applicable service procedure requires it.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Measured pressures are within the model-specific range for the current operating condition.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Follow the manufacturer diagnostic procedure for pressure, sensor, or refrigerant-circuit faults.

requiredMeasurements.name: Outdoor ambient temperature
requiredMeasurements.description: Ambient condition used when interpreting pressure-related operation.

requiredMeasurements.name: Fan operation status
requiredMeasurements.description: Outdoor fan operation and airflow condition observed by qualified personnel.

requiredMeasurements.name: Refrigerant pressure
requiredMeasurements.description: Model-specific pressure reading where the service manual requires pressure confirmation.

safetyNotes: Electrical, compressor, inverter, refrigerant, and chiller protection checks must be performed by a qualified technician.
Keep safety switches, protection inputs, current protection, pressure protection, flow protection, and controller safeguards active during diagnosis.
De-energize and lock out equipment where the service procedure requires it, and follow the manufacturer manual and local safety rules.

source.notes: Seeded deterministic diagnostic guidance. Not manually page-verified.
limitations: Use as preliminary diagnostic guidance only.
Verify against the exact service manual for the installed equipment family before final conclusion.

entries.title: GMV preliminary alarm E3
entries.meaning: Preliminary diagnostic guidance for a Gree GMV E3 alarm. Some Gree equipment families associate E3-style alarms with low-pressure or refrigerant-circuit protection, but the exact meaning must be verified against the service manual for the installed model.
likelyCauses: Low-pressure or refrigerant-circuit protection condition reported by the controls.
Restricted refrigerant flow, insufficient heat exchange, or abnormal operating condition.
Pressure sensor, wiring, or controller input issue requiring qualified diagnosis.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm operating context
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Record the installed model, operating mode, indoor load condition, ambient condition, and the displayed E3 code.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: The equipment identity and operating context are documented before diagnosis continues.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Stop classification and obtain complete system information.

diagnosticSteps.title: Inspect airflow and heat exchange
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should inspect indoor and outdoor airflow paths, coil condition, filter condition, and fan operation according to the service procedure.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Airflow and heat exchange paths are suitable for the active operating mode.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Correct airflow or heat-exchange restrictions through qualified service procedures.

diagnosticSteps.title: Check pressure-related inputs
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should compare pressure-related readings and sensor wiring against the exact model service manual.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Pressure readings and sensor input behavior match model-specific expectations.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Follow the manufacturer diagnostic path for sensor, wiring, or refrigerant-circuit issues.

requiredMeasurements.name: Indoor return air temperature
requiredMeasurements.description: Return air condition used to interpret low-load or heat-exchange behavior.

requiredMeasurements.name: Outdoor ambient temperature
requiredMeasurements.description: Outdoor condition used when interpreting refrigerant-circuit operation.

requiredMeasurements.name: Pressure-related sensor reading
requiredMeasurements.description: Sensor or pressure reading required by the model-specific service procedure.

safetyNotes: Electrical, compressor, inverter, refrigerant, and chiller protection checks must be performed by a qualified technician.
Keep safety switches, protection inputs, current protection, pressure protection, flow protection, and controller safeguards active during diagnosis.
De-energize and lock out equipment where the service procedure requires it, and follow the manufacturer manual and local safety rules.

source.notes: Seeded deterministic diagnostic guidance. Not manually page-verified.
limitations: Use as preliminary diagnostic guidance only.
Verify against the exact service manual for the installed equipment family before final conclusion.

entries.title: GMV preliminary alarm E4
entries.meaning: Preliminary diagnostic guidance for a Gree GMV E4 alarm. Some Gree equipment families associate E4-style alarms with discharge temperature or compressor protection, but the exact meaning must be verified against the service manual for the installed model.
likelyCauses: Compressor discharge temperature or compressor protection condition reported by the controls.
Abnormal refrigerant circuit operation, heat-exchange issue, or sensor input issue.
Compressor, inverter, or control-board condition requiring qualified diagnosis.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm alarm history
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Record the displayed E4 code, operating mode, alarm recurrence, outdoor ambient condition, and any companion controller diagnostics.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Alarm context and recurrence are documented before component-level diagnosis.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Collect controller history and exact model information before continuing.

diagnosticSteps.title: Check temperature-related readings
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should compare discharge-temperature and related sensor readings against the exact service manual.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Temperature readings are plausible and within model-specific expectations for the operating state.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Follow the manufacturer diagnostic path for sensor, wiring, or refrigerant-circuit issues.

diagnosticSteps.title: Inspect compressor operating condition
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should inspect compressor and inverter operating indicators using approved procedures.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Compressor and inverter operation is consistent with the expected operating state.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Escalate to qualified compressor or inverter diagnosis according to the manufacturer procedure.

requiredMeasurements.name: Discharge temperature
requiredMeasurements.description: Compressor discharge temperature reading where available through the controller or service procedure.

requiredMeasurements.name: Outdoor ambient temperature
requiredMeasurements.description: Ambient temperature used to interpret compressor and heat-rejection operation.

requiredMeasurements.name: Operating current
requiredMeasurements.description: Measured compressor or inverter input current where the service procedure requires it.

safetyNotes: Electrical, compressor, inverter, refrigerant, and chiller protection checks must be performed by a qualified technician.
Keep safety switches, protection inputs, current protection, pressure protection, flow protection, and controller safeguards active during diagnosis.
De-energize and lock out equipment where the service procedure requires it, and follow the manufacturer manual and local safety rules.

source.notes: Seeded deterministic diagnostic guidance. Not manually page-verified.
limitations: Use as preliminary diagnostic guidance only.
Verify against the exact service manual for the installed equipment family before final conclusion.

entries.title: GMV preliminary alarm E5
entries.meaning: Preliminary diagnostic guidance for a Gree GMV E5 alarm. Some Gree equipment families associate E5-style alarms with electrical or current-related protection, but the exact meaning must be verified against the service manual for the installed model.
likelyCauses: Electrical supply, current, or inverter protection condition reported by the outdoor unit controls.
Abnormal voltage, phase condition, terminal connection, or operating current.
Compressor, inverter, or control-board issue requiring qualified diagnosis.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm electrical context
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Record the model, displayed E5 code, power supply type, recent power events, and active operating mode.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Electrical context and displayed code are confirmed before diagnosis continues.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Stop classification and obtain complete electrical and model information.

diagnosticSteps.title: Measure supply condition
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should measure supply voltage and phase condition at the service point using approved procedures.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Supply values are within the equipment nameplate and local electrical requirements.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Correct supply or wiring issues through qualified electrical service.

diagnosticSteps.title: Inspect current-related wiring
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Inspect compressor, inverter, current-sensing, and control-board connections for visible damage, loose terminals, or moisture ingress.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Connections are secure and no visible damage is found.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Repair wiring or component issues according to the manufacturer procedure.

requiredMeasurements.name: Supply voltage
requiredMeasurements.description: Measured line voltage and phase balance at the unit service point.

requiredMeasurements.name: Operating current
requiredMeasurements.description: Measured compressor or inverter input current where the service procedure requires it.

requiredMeasurements.name: Terminal condition
requiredMeasurements.description: Observed condition of relevant power and control terminals by qualified personnel.

safetyNotes: Electrical, compressor, inverter, refrigerant, and chiller protection checks must be performed by a qualified technician.
Keep safety switches, protection inputs, current protection, pressure protection, flow protection, and controller safeguards active during diagnosis.
De-energize and lock out equipment where the service procedure requires it, and follow the manufacturer manual and local safety rules.

Verify against the exact service manual for the installed equipment family before final conclusion.
